desc: Athens pairs world-class antiquities with lively modern districts, rooftop dining, and easy Aegean add-ons. It is busiest in late spring and early autumn, while midsummer remains active but hotter and less comfortable for long daytime site walks.
hls: Acropolis and Acropolis Museum in one compact historic core
Layered neighborhoods from Plaka and Monastiraki to edgy Psyrri
Strong food scene: meze, grilled seafood, and modern Greek kitchens
Excellent sunset viewpoints on Lycabettus and Filopappou Hill
Easy ferry and mainland day trips (Aegina, Hydra, Cape Sounion)
Great value shoulder-season city break with mild weather

todo.name: Acropolis + Acropolis Museum
todo.desc: Start early at the Acropolis, then continue to the museum to understand the Parthenon sculptures and city history.

todo.name: Ancient Agora and Temple of Hephaestus
todo.desc: Walk the political and commercial heart of ancient Athens, one of the best-preserved classical sites.

todo.name: Plaka and Anafiotika wander
todo.desc: Explore whitewashed lanes, neoclassical houses, and stairways under the Acropolis cliffs.

todo.name: Central Market (Varvakios) lunch crawl
todo.desc: Sample olives, cured meats, spices, and casual tavernas around Athens' main market district.

todo.name: Sunset at Lycabettus Hill
todo.desc: Ride or walk up for panoramic views over the Acropolis and Saronic Gulf at golden hour.

todo.name: Cape Sounion half-day trip
todo.desc: Visit the Temple of Poseidon on dramatic sea cliffs, especially rewarding near sunset.

todo.name: National Archaeological Museum
todo.desc: See major Mycenaean, Cycladic, and classical collections for deeper historical context.

todo.name: Evening in Psyrri or Koukaki
todo.desc: Try contemporary tavernas, wine bars, and live music in two of the city's most social districts.

wifi.note: Generally strong in central stays and coworking cafés; older buildings can have weaker in-room signal in upper floors.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Return fares are often ~£70–£170 off-peak, ~£170–£280 in shoulder demand, and ~£280+ around Easter, high summer, and holiday peaks.
prac.visa: Greece is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Acropolis and museum timed entries sell out during spring and autumn peaks — prebook where possible.
Heatwaves in July–August can significantly affect comfort/safety for exposed archaeological walks.
City protests/strikes occasionally affect transport; check local advisories for short visits.

sweet: April to June and September to October usually give the best balance of weather, site comfort, and crowd levels.
